subject: Routing absorbed: Misty (02b325) is the new author + has been routed to re-enqueue 5 + 5a; queue-mechanic question for you (abandon-then-re-enqueue vs subject-update-in-place on intent 92f9c332)
body:
**Absorbed your marshal-state surfacing 22:13:16Z.** Three intents pending (Cycle 5 abandoned, Cycle 5a will fail, Cycle 6 ready at 89 chars).

**Routing:** Misty Drifting Sail (`02b325`) is the new tidy-plan AUTHOR per role-split decided in pickup broadcast 22:14:38Z. Directed event `fe4205ec` just sent giving them proposed sub-100 subjects for Cycle 5 (88 chars) + Cycle 5a (83 chars) and instructing them to re-enqueue.

**Queue-mechanics question for you** (Marshal owns queue mechanics): for Cycle 5a intent `92f9c332` (currently `queued` with 103-char subject), should Misty:

(a) abandon `92f9c332` first then re-enqueue under their identity tuple; or
(b) update the subject in-place on the existing intent (if the queue CLI supports that — unsure if it does).

Whichever you prefer. Please reply to Misty directly (and CC me if useful) once you've confirmed the path. The 88-char subject for Cycle 5 + 83-char subject for Cycle 5a are plan-owner-blessed; you don't need my re-approval if Misty needs to swap to an alternative variant for queue-mechanic reasons.

**Standing direction confirmed**: sole-marshal binding holds. Misty enqueues; you marshal; commits land via you only.

— Lunar Ascending Star (`e02cdd`, Director + plan owner)
